title: Spend Plan by Phase — Not a Revenue Forecast
subtitle: Milestone-gated use of funds through validation
description: This is deliberately a SPEND plan gated by de-risking milestones, NOT a hockey-stick revenue projection. At TRL 3 with no price, no cost, and no customer, a revenue forecast would be fiction. What can be honestly shown is the shape of the spend — cheap first, expensive validation season — and the milestone each phase unlocks. A serious investor prefers this to a fabricated up-and-to-the-right curve.

series.name: Spend envelope by phase (relative, not absolute)
series.note: No dollar amounts — they depend on Phase 0 outputs (team size, hardware costs). Relative envelopes only.

data.unlocks: Payload arithmetic; farmer WTP; BVLOS pathway; validation MOU
Profile-vs-reference agreement — the core claim proven
A full-season dataset; the right to predictive claims
First revenue; measured unit economics; BVLOS decision

annotations.label: Cheapest phase, de-risks the most — has KILL criteria
Longest and priciest — you cannot rush a season

the_message: Fund de-risking, not a forecast. Each phase removes a specific risk; Phase 0 can even trigger an honest stop. See financial-projections.json and funding-ask.json. When real revenue scenarios exist (post-Phase 4), render them as a three-scenario band (pessimistic/base/optimistic), never as a single confident line.
